test_cases.description: Provider registry stores GitHub entry with all required fields and default scopes of 'user:email'.
preconditions: Server is initialized with default GitHub provider configuration
No URL overrides are configured for GitHub
test_cases.input: Call GET /api/v1/auth/providers
test_cases.expected: Response body contains providers array with one entry: {name: 'github', authorize_url: 'https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize', scopes: 'user:email'}; token_url and client_secret are NOT present in response
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert resp.status == 200
providers = resp.body.providers
assert len(providers) == 1
github = providers[0]
assert github.name == 'github'
assert github.authorize_url == 'https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize'
assert github.scopes == 'user:email'
assert 'client_secret' not in github
assert 'token_url' not in github

test_cases.description: Provider registry uses configured URL overrides instead of defaults when provided.
preconditions: Server is initialized with GitHub provider configuration that includes overrides: authorize_url='https://custom.example.com/authorize', token_url='https://custom.example.com/token', userinfo_url='https://custom.example.com/userinfo'
test_cases.input: Call GET /api/v1/auth/providers
test_cases.expected: Response contains github provider entry with authorize_url equal to 'https://custom.example.com/authorize'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert resp.status == 200
github = find(resp.body.providers, name='github')
assert github.authorize_url == 'https://custom.example.com/authorize'

test_cases.description: A new provider can be added to the registry and appears in the providers list without modifying authentication flow code.
preconditions: Server is initialized with GitHub provider and an additional 'gitlab' provider registered in the registry
No code changes to the authentication flow handler are made
test_cases.input: Call GET /api/v1/auth/providers
test_cases.expected: Response contains a providers array with entries for both 'github' and 'gitlab'; authentication flow code path is unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert resp.status == 200
names = [p.name for p in resp.body.providers]
assert 'github' in names
assert 'gitlab' in names

test_cases.description: GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with providers array containing name, authorize_url, and scopes; no secrets exposed.
preconditions: Server is running with GitHub provider configured
No authentication token is provided (public endpoint)
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/auth/providers (no auth header)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; JSON body has shape {providers: [{name: string, authorize_url: string, scopes: string}]}; no client_secret or token_url fields present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert resp.status == 200
assert 'providers' in resp.body
for p in resp.body.providers:
  assert p.name != ''
  assert p.authorize_url != ''
  assert p.scopes != ''
  assert 'client_secret' not in p
  assert 'token_url' not in p

test_cases.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with valid provider, expires, and allowlisted redirect_uri completes the full login flow and returns user object plus API key.
preconditions: Server running in dev mode (no external_url configured)
GitHub provider registered in provider registry
GitHub OAuth code exchange endpoint is mocked to return valid user info (login='testuser', email='test@example.com', id='12345')
SQLite database initialized with users and api_keys tables
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'valid-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/callback', expires: 30}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body contains user object with id (UUID v4), username='testuser', email='test@example.com', status='active', provider='github'; body contains api_key object with key_id (8 alphanumeric chars), secret (32 alphanumeric chars), token matching 'af_<key_id>_<secret>', expires_at approximately 30 days from now; all DB operations committed in single transaction
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'valid-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/callback', expires:30})
assert resp.status == 200
user = resp.body.user
assert is_uuid_v4(user.id)
assert user.username == 'testuser'
assert user.email == 'test@example.com'
assert user.status == 'active'
assert user.provider == 'github'
key = resp.body.api_key
assert len(key.key_id) == 8
assert key.key_id.isalnum()
assert len(key.secret) == 32
assert key.secret.isalnum()
assert key.token == 'af_' + key.key_id + '_' + key.secret
assert key.user_id == user.id
diff = parse_iso8601(key.expires_at) - now()
assert 29.9 < diff.days < 30.1
# Verify DB state
db_user = db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', user.id)
assert db_user != None
db_key = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', key.key_id)
assert db_key != None
assert db_key.secret_hash == sha256(key.secret)

test_cases.description: Callback creates a new user with status 'active' and UUID v4 id when no user exists for (provider, provider_id).
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return login='newuser', email='new@example.com', id='99999'
SQLite database has no user row for (provider='github', provider_id='99999')
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'new-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; new user row inserted in users table with status='active', a UUID v4 id, provider_id='99999', created_at and updated_at set
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'new-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 200
user = resp.body.user
assert is_uuid_v4(user.id)
assert user.status == 'active'
db_user = db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', user.id)
assert db_user.provider_id == '99999'
assert db_user.status == 'active'
assert db_user.created_at != None

test_cases.description: Callback updates username and email from provider response for existing user without changing provider_id; updated_at bumped only if value changes.
preconditions: Existing user row: id='user-uuid-1', username='olduser', email='old@example.com', provider='github', provider_id='12345', updated_at=T0
GitHub provider mocked to return login='newuser', email='new@example.com', id='12345'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'code-123', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; user row updated with username='newuser', email='new@example.com'; provider_id remains '12345'; updated_at > T0 because username and email changed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
T0 = db.query('SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE provider_id = ?', '12345').updated_at
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'code-123', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 200
db_user = db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE provider_id = ?', '12345')
assert db_user.username == 'newuser'
assert db_user.email == 'new@example.com'
assert db_user.provider_id == '12345'
assert db_user.updated_at > T0

test_cases.description: New API key is generated with 8-char base62 key_id, 32-char base62 secret from crypto/rand; SHA-256 hash stored; expires_at computed correctly; token format is af_<key_id>_<secret>.
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return login='keyuser', email='key@example.com', id='55555'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'code-x', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb', expires: 60}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; api_key.key_id is exactly 8 alphanumeric chars; api_key.secret is exactly 32 alphanumeric chars; api_key.token == 'af_' + key_id + '_' + secret; db api_keys row has secret_hash == SHA-256(secret); expires_at is approximately 60 days from now
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'code-x', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb', expires:60})
assert resp.status == 200
k = resp.body.api_key
assert len(k.key_id) == 8
assert matches_regex(k.key_id, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$')
assert len(k.secret) == 32
assert matches_regex(k.secret, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$')
assert k.token == 'af_' + k.key_id + '_' + k.secret
db_key = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', k.key_id)
assert db_key.secret_hash == sha256_hex(k.secret)
diff_days = (parse_iso8601(k.expires_at) - now()).total_seconds() / 86400
assert 59.9 < diff_days < 60.1
assert k.expires_at != None

test_cases.description: Login revokes the existing active key before creating a new key; if no active key exists, creation still proceeds without error.
preconditions: Existing user for (provider='github', provider_id='77777') with one active api_keys row (revoked_at=null, expires_at=null)
GitHub provider mocked to return login='revokeuser', email='r@example.com', id='77777'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'code-r', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; old key row in api_keys has revoked_at set (non-null); new key row exists with revoked_at=null; both operations in same transaction
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
old_key_id = db.query('SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL', existing_user_id).key_id
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'code-r', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 200
new_key_id = resp.body.api_key.key_id
assert new_key_id != old_key_id
old_row = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', old_key_id)
assert old_row.revoked_at != None
new_row = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', new_key_id)
assert new_row.revoked_at == None

test_cases.description: In dev mode (no external_url), any http://localhost redirect_uri origin is allowed regardless of port; non-localhost hosts are rejected.
preconditions: Server running in dev mode (external_url not set in config)
GitHub provider mocked to return valid user info
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with redirect_uri='http://localhost:8080/cb' (then with 'http://localhost:3000/cb')
test_cases.expected: Both localhost URIs return HTTP 200; requests with redirect_uri='http://otherhost:3000/cb' return HTTP 400
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c1', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:8080/cb'})
assert resp1.status == 200
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c2', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp2.status == 200
resp3 = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c3', redirect_uri:'http://otherhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp3.status == 400

test_cases.description: In production mode (external_url set), only redirect_uri origins exactly matching external_url scheme+host+port are allowed.
preconditions: Server running in production mode with external_url='https://app.example.com'
GitHub provider mocked to return valid user info
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with redirect_uri='https://app.example.com/callback' (matching) and 'https://app.example.com:8443/callback' (non-matching)
test_cases.expected: Matching origin (https://app.example.com) returns HTTP 200; non-matching port (https://app.example.com:8443) returns HTTP 400
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp_ok = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c1', redirect_uri:'https://app.example.com/callback'})
assert resp_ok.status == 200
resp_bad = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c2', redirect_uri:'https://app.example.com:8443/callback'})
assert resp_bad.status == 400

test_cases.description: Allowlist check extracts only scheme+host+port from redirect_uri; path component is not considered.
preconditions: Server running in production mode with external_url='https://app.example.com'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with redirect_uri='https://app.example.com/some/path?foo=bar'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 returned because origin matches; path and query are not considered in the check
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c1', redirect_uri:'https://app.example.com/some/path?foo=bar'})
assert resp.status == 200
# Also verify a URI with same path but different host is rejected
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c2', redirect_uri:'https://evil.example.com/some/path?foo=bar'})
assert resp2.status == 400

test_cases.description: Admin POST /api/v1/users creates a user with status='active', UUID v4 id, no API key, and returns HTTP 201 with user object excluding provider_id.
preconditions: Server running with admin token configured
SQLite database initialized with users and api_keys tables
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/users with admin token and body: {username: 'adminuser', email: 'admin@example.com', full_name: 'Admin User', provider: 'github', provider_id: 'ext-001'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; response body contains id (UUID v4), username='adminuser', email='admin@example.com', status='active', provider='github', created_at, updated_at; provider_id NOT in response; no row in api_keys table for this user
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'adminuser', email:'admin@example.com', full_name:'Admin User', provider:'github', provider_id:'ext-001'})
assert resp.status == 201
u = resp.body
assert is_uuid_v4(u.id)
assert u.username == 'adminuser'
assert u.email == 'admin@example.com'
assert u.status == 'active'
assert u.provider == 'github'
assert 'provider_id' not in u
assert u.created_at != None
assert u.updated_at != None
keys = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?', u.id)
assert len(keys) == 0

test_cases.description: Admin POST /api/v1/users accepts any non-empty provider string and logs a warning for unregistered providers but still creates the user.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/users with admin token and body: {username: 'provtest', email: 'p@example.com', provider: 'unknownprovider', provider_id: 'ext-999'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; user record created with provider='unknownprovider'; server logs a warning about unregistered provider (verifiable via log output)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'provtest', email:'p@example.com', provider:'unknownprovider', provider_id:'ext-999'})
assert resp.status == 201
assert resp.body.provider == 'unknownprovider'
db_user = db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?', 'provtest')
assert db_user != None
# Log verification: assert server log contains warning about 'unknownprovider' not being a registered provider

test_cases.description: Admin POST /api/v1/users enforces username alphanumeric+hyphen, max 39 chars, and non-empty provider_id.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/users with invalid username 'bad_user!' and with empty provider_id
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 for invalid username; HTTP 400 for empty provider_id
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'bad_user!', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p1'})
assert resp1.status == 400
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'validuser', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:''})
assert resp2.status == 400
resp3 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'a'*40, email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p2'})
assert resp3.status == 400

test_cases.description: Admin POST /api/v1/users enforces case-insensitive username uniqueness and uniqueness of (provider, provider_id).
preconditions: Existing user with username='TestUser', provider='github', provider_id='ext-100'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/users with username='testuser' (same as existing, different case) or with same provider+provider_id pair
test_cases.expected: HTTP 409 for case-insensitive username collision; HTTP 409 for duplicate (provider, provider_id)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'testuser', email:'e@e.com', provider:'gitlab', provider_id:'ext-200'})
assert resp1.status == 409
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'differentuser', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'ext-100'})
assert resp2.status == 409

test_cases.description: Admin GET /api/v1/users returns HTTP 200 with all user records ordered by created_at ASC; provider_id omitted from each entry.
preconditions: Two existing users in DB: user A created at T1, user B created at T2 (T1 < T2)
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/users with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body.users is an array with user A first, user B second; each entry has id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, created_at, updated_at; provider_id NOT present in any entry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 200
users = resp.body.users
assert len(users) == 2
assert users[0].created_at < users[1].created_at
for u in users:
  assert 'id' in u
  assert 'username' in u
  assert 'email' in u
  assert 'status' in u
  assert 'provider' in u
  assert 'created_at' in u
  assert 'updated_at' in u
  assert 'provider_id' not in u

test_cases.description: Admin GET /api/v1/users returns HTTP 200 with empty users array when no users exist.
preconditions: SQLite database has no rows in users table
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/users with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body is {users: []}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.users == []

test_cases.description: Admin GET /api/v1/users/:id returns full user object with provider_id and team_memberships array.
preconditions: User has one team membership: {team_id: 'team-1', team_name: 'Alpha', role: 'member'} in team_memberships table
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/users/user-uuid-1 with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body includes all user fields including provider_id='ext-111'; team_memberships array contains one entry with team_id='team-1', team_name='Alpha', role='member'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-1', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 200
u = resp.body
assert u.id == 'user-uuid-1'
assert u.provider_id == 'ext-111'
assert 'team_memberships' in u
assert len(u.team_memberships) == 1
assert u.team_memberships[0].team_id == 'team-1'
assert u.team_memberships[0].team_name == 'Alpha'
assert u.team_memberships[0].role == 'member'

test_cases.description: Authenticated user can update their own full_name; null or empty string clears the field; updated_at bumped only when value changes.
preconditions: User 'user-uuid-2' authenticated with their own API key
test_cases.input: PUT /api/v1/users/user-uuid-2 with user's own key and body: {full_name: 'New Name'}; then body: {full_name: null}
test_cases.expected: First request: HTTP 200, full_name='New Name', updated_at > T0; Second request: HTTP 200, full_name=null, updated_at updated again; provider_id included; team_memberships NOT included
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
T0 = db.query('SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-2').updated_at
resp1 = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-2', headers={'Authorization':'user-key'}, body={full_name:'New Name'})
assert resp1.status == 200
assert resp1.body.full_name == 'New Name'
assert resp1.body.updated_at > T0
assert resp1.body.provider_id != None
assert 'team_memberships' not in resp1.body

resp2 = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-2', headers={'Authorization':'user-key'}, body={full_name:null})
assert resp2.status == 200
assert resp2.body.full_name == null

test_cases.description: Admin can update status on any user; updated_at bumped only when value changes.
test_cases.input: PUT /api/v1/users/user-uuid-3 with admin token and body: {status: 'blocked'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body has status='blocked', updated_at > T0; provider_id included; team_memberships NOT included
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
T0 = db.query('SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-3').updated_at
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-3', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={status:'blocked'})
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.status == 'blocked'
assert resp.body.updated_at > T0
assert resp.body.provider_id != None
assert 'team_memberships' not in resp.body

test_cases.description: PUT /api/v1/users/:id with no recognized fields or identical values returns HTTP 200 with unmodified user object; updated_at not bumped.
preconditions: User 'user-uuid-4' with full_name='Same Name', updated_at=T0, authenticated with their own key
test_cases.input: PUT /api/v1/users/user-uuid-4 with body: {full_name: 'Same Name'} (no change)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response body matches current stored user object; updated_at equals T0 (not bumped)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
T0 = db.query('SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-4').updated_at
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-4', headers={'Authorization':'user-key'}, body={full_name:'Same Name'})
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.full_name == 'Same Name'
assert resp.body.updated_at == T0
# Also test with empty body
resp2 = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-4', headers={'Authorization':'user-key'}, body={})
assert resp2.status == 200
assert resp2.body.updated_at == T0

test_cases.description: Admin updating their own user record operates with admin-level permissions and can set status on themselves.
preconditions: Admin user 'admin-uuid-1' authenticated with admin token
test_cases.input: PUT /api/v1/users/admin-uuid-1 with admin token and body: {status: 'blocked'}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; admin's own status updated to 'blocked'; no 403 error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/admin-uuid-1', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={status:'blocked'})
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.status == 'blocked'

test_cases.description: Admin GET /api/v1/keys returns all API keys across all users ordered by created_at ASC; no secret field included.
preconditions: Two users: user A has one key created at T1, user B has one key created at T2 (T1 < T2)
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/keys with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body.keys contains both keys ordered T1 then T2; each entry has key_id, user_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at; secret NOT present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 200
keys = resp.body.keys
assert len(keys) == 2
assert keys[0].created_at < keys[1].created_at
for k in keys:
  assert 'key_id' in k
  assert 'user_id' in k
  assert 'created_at' in k
  assert 'expires_at' in k
  assert 'revoked_at' in k
  assert 'secret' not in k
  assert 'token' not in k

test_cases.description: User GET /api/v1/keys returns all historical keys for the requesting user (including expired and revoked) ordered by created_at ASC; no secret.
preconditions: User 'user-uuid-5' with own API key; db has 3 api_keys rows for this user: one active, one expired, one revoked
Another user exists with their own keys
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/keys with user-uuid-5's own API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body.keys contains exactly 3 entries (all for user-uuid-5) ordered by created_at ASC; other user's keys not included; secret NOT in any entry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={'Authorization':'user-5-key'})
assert resp.status == 200
keys = resp.body.keys
assert len(keys) == 3
for k in keys:
  assert k.user_id == 'user-uuid-5'
  assert 'secret' not in k
assert keys[0].created_at <= keys[1].created_at <= keys[2].created_at

test_cases.description: Key listing responses never include a computed status or is_active field; consumers derive validity from expires_at and revoked_at.
preconditions: At least one key exists in api_keys table
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/keys with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; no entry in keys array contains 'status', 'is_active', or 'is_valid' fields
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 200
for k in resp.body.keys:
  assert 'status' not in k
  assert 'is_active' not in k
  assert 'is_valid' not in k

test_cases.description: POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh generates a new secret, stores SHA-256 hash, recalculates expires_at, returns key with plaintext secret and token.
preconditions: User 'user-uuid-6' with active key 'key-abc123' (90-day expiry, created 10 days ago, not revoked)
User authenticated with their own API key
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/keys/key-abc123/refresh with user-uuid-6's own API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; response has key_id='key-abc123', new secret (32 alphanumeric chars), token='af_key-abc123_<new_secret>', revoked_at=null; db secret_hash updated to SHA-256 of new secret; expires_at approximately 90 days from now
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
old_hash = db.query('SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-abc123').secret_hash
resp = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-abc123/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'user-6-key'})
assert resp.status == 200
k = resp.body
assert k.key_id == 'key-abc123'
assert len(k.secret) == 32
assert matches_regex(k.secret, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$')
assert k.token == 'af_key-abc123_' + k.secret
assert k.revoked_at == None
new_hash = db.query('SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-abc123').secret_hash
assert new_hash != old_hash
assert new_hash == sha256_hex(k.secret)
diff_days = (parse_iso8601(k.expires_at) - now()).total_seconds() / 86400
assert 89.9 < diff_days < 90.1

test_cases.description: Refreshing an expired (non-revoked) key is permitted; new expires_at is calculated using original expiry duration from now.
preconditions: User 'user-uuid-7' with expired key 'key-expired1' (30-day original expiry, expires_at in the past, revoked_at=null)
User authenticated with their own API key
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/keys/key-expired1/refresh with user-uuid-7's own API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; new expires_at is approximately 30 days from now; new secret returned in plaintext
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-expired1/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'user-7-key'})
assert resp.status == 200
k = resp.body
assert k.key_id == 'key-expired1'
diff_days = (parse_iso8601(k.expires_at) - now()).total_seconds() / 86400
assert 29.9 < diff_days < 30.1
assert len(k.secret) == 32

test_cases.description: Admin can refresh a key belonging to a blocked user; user status does not prevent admin key management.
preconditions: User 'user-uuid-8' with status='blocked' and active key 'key-blocked1'
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/keys/key-blocked1/refresh with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; key refreshed successfully; new secret returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-blocked1/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.key_id == 'key-blocked1'
assert len(resp.body.secret) == 32

test_cases.description: Refresh endpoint does not allow caller to override expiry duration; original duration is always reused.
preconditions: User with 30-day expiry key 'key-30day', not revoked
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/keys/key-30day/refresh with body: {expires: 90} (attempt to override duration)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; expires_at is recalculated as 30 days from now (original duration), not 90 days; body field 'expires' in request is ignored
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-30day/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'user-key'}, body={expires:90})
assert resp.status == 200
diff_days = (parse_iso8601(resp.body.expires_at) - now()).total_seconds() / 86400
assert 29.9 < diff_days < 30.1  # 30 days, not 90

test_cases.description: DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id sets revoked_at on first revocation and returns HTTP 204 with no body.
preconditions: User authenticated with their own key
test_cases.input: DELETE /api/v1/keys/key-torevoke with user-uuid-9's own API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204; no response body; db row for key-torevoke has revoked_at set to a non-null timestamp
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/keys/key-torevoke', headers={'Authorization':'user-9-key'})
assert resp.status == 204
assert resp.body == None or resp.body == ''
db_key = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-torevoke')
assert db_key.revoked_at != None

test_cases.description: DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id is idempotent; revoking an already-revoked key returns HTTP 204 without updating revoked_at again.
preconditions: Key 'key-alreadyrevoked' exists in api_keys table with revoked_at=T_REVOKE
test_cases.input: DELETE /api/v1/keys/key-alreadyrevoked with admin token (key already revoked)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204; revoked_at in db remains equal to T_REVOKE (not updated)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
T_REVOKE = db.query('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-alreadyrevoked').revoked_at
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/keys/key-alreadyrevoked', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 204
db_key = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-alreadyrevoked')
assert db_key.revoked_at == T_REVOKE  # unchanged

test_cases.description: Admin can revoke a key belonging to a blocked user; user status does not prevent admin key revocation.
preconditions: User 'user-blocked-uuid' with status='blocked' and active key 'key-blockeduser'
test_cases.input: DELETE /api/v1/keys/key-blockeduser with admin token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204; key-blockeduser.revoked_at is now set (non-null)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/keys/key-blockeduser', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 204
db_key = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-blockeduser')
assert db_key.revoked_at != None

test_cases.description: Key generation code uses crypto/rand as entropy source; no calls to math/rand or deterministic generators in the key generation path.
preconditions: Access to source code in the key generation module
test_cases.input: Static analysis or code inspection of key generation functions
test_cases.expected: Source code imports and uses 'crypto/rand' for key_id and secret generation; no import or call to 'math/rand' found in the key generation code path
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
# Static analysis check
source = read_file('internal/keys/generate.go')  # or equivalent path
assert 'crypto/rand' in source
assert 'math/rand' not in source
# Runtime check: generated key_id values across 1000 invocations should have high entropy (no collisions)
ids = set(generate_key_id() for _ in range(1000))
assert len(ids) == 1000  # no collisions expected with crypto/rand

test_cases.description: Only SHA-256 hash of secret is stored in api_keys table; plaintext secret never appears in any database column.
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return valid user info
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback to create a new key; inspect all columns of the resulting api_keys row
test_cases.expected: api_keys row has secret_hash equal to SHA-256(returned_secret); no column contains the plaintext secret string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
plaintext_secret = resp.body.api_key.secret
key_id = resp.body.api_key.key_id
db_row = db.query('SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', key_id)
assert db_row.secret_hash == sha256_hex(plaintext_secret)
# Verify plaintext not stored anywhere
for col_name, col_val in db_row.items():
  if col_name != 'secret_hash':
    assert plaintext_secret not in str(col_val)

test_cases.description: key_id is exactly 8 base62 chars, secret is exactly 32 base62 chars; token format is af_<key_id>_<secret>.
test_cases.input: Generate 100 API keys via POST /api/v1/auth/callback or key generation function directly
test_cases.expected: Every key_id matches regex ^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$; every secret matches ^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$; every token matches ^af_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for i in range(100):
  resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c'+str(i), redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
  k = resp.body.api_key
  assert matches_regex(k.key_id, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$')
  assert matches_regex(k.secret, '^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$')
  assert matches_regex(k.token, '^af_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$')

test_cases.description: Username validation accepts alphanumeric+hyphen chars up to 39 chars; rejects any invalid chars or length > 39.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/users with various username values: valid 'alice-bob', invalid 'alice_bob', invalid 'alice bob', invalid 'a'*40
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 for 'alice-bob'; HTTP 400 for 'alice_bob', 'alice bob', and 40-char username
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'alice-bob', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p1'})
assert r1.status == 201
r2 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'alice_bob', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p2'})
assert r2.status == 400
r3 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'alice bob', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p3'})
assert r3.status == 400
r4 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'a'*40, email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p4'})
assert r4.status == 400

test_cases.description: Username is stored as-is (case preserved) but uniqueness enforced by lowercased comparison.
test_cases.input: POST /api/v1/users with username='Alice'; then POST /api/v1/users with username='alice'
test_cases.expected: First request returns HTTP 201 with username='Alice'; second request returns HTTP 409 (case-insensitive duplicate)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'Alice', email:'a@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p10'})
assert r1.status == 201
assert r1.body.username == 'Alice'
r2 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'alice', email:'b@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p11'})
assert r2.status == 409

test_cases.description: updated_at is updated only when at least one field value changes; identical values on PUT do not bump updated_at.
preconditions: User authenticated with their own key
test_cases.input: PUT /api/v1/users/user-uuid-10 with body: {full_name: 'Unchanged'} (no actual change)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; updated_at in response and in DB equals T0 (not bumped)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
T0 = db.query('SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-10').updated_at
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-10', headers={'Authorization':'user-10-key'}, body={full_name:'Unchanged'})
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.updated_at == T0
db_val = db.query('SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-10').updated_at
assert db_val == T0

property_tests.description: For any user, at most one API key row is simultaneously non-revoked and non-expired (active) at any point in time.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any user_id in the users table, after any sequence of callback logins (each generating a new key), query api_keys for all rows where user_id matches, revoked_at IS NULL, and (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())
property_tests.invariant_check: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = :user_id AND revoked_at IS NULL AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); assert result <= 1

property_tests.description: For any api_keys row, secret_hash equals the SHA-256 hash of the secret returned at creation or refresh; plaintext secret never appears in any database column.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any key created or refreshed, capture the plaintext secret from the response; then inspect all columns of the corresponding api_keys row
property_tests.invariant_check: db_row = SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = :key_id; assert db_row.secret_hash == sha256_hex(plaintext_secret); for each column_value in db_row.values(): assert plaintext_secret not in str(column_value) or column_name == 'secret_hash'

property_tests.description: Once a key's revoked_at is set, no subsequent refresh or re-authentication clears it; revocation is permanent.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any revoked key (revoked_at != null), attempt POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh and a new callback login that would create a new key for the same user; then re-query the revoked key row
property_tests.invariant_check: revoked_key_row = SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = :key_id AND revoked_at IS NOT NULL; attempt refresh -> expect HTTP 404; re-query: assert revoked_key_row.revoked_at is still non-null and unchanged

property_tests.description: No two rows in the users table have usernames that are equal when both are lowercased.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any pair of distinct user rows in the users table (exhaustive check after any sequence of user creation operations)
property_tests.invariant_check: rows = SELECT id, LOWER(username) as lower_username FROM users; assert len(set(r.lower_username for r in rows)) == len(rows)  # no duplicates in lowercased set

property_tests.description: The OAuth callback login transaction is atomic: either all three operations (user upsert, key revocation, new key creation) are committed, or none are; no partial state is observable.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any callback invocation that reaches the DB write phase: inject a failure at each step (after upsert, after revocation, after key insert) and observe the resulting database state
property_tests.invariant_check: For each injected failure point: db_state_before = snapshot(users, api_keys); invoke callback with injected failure; db_state_after = snapshot(users, api_keys); assert db_state_after == db_state_before  # no partial writes committed

property_tests.description: Every key_id and secret value is generated using crypto/rand; the key generation loop terminates within a bounded number of iterations (no infinite retry).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Instrument the key generation function to count calls to crypto/rand.Read; for any single key generation invocation
property_tests.invariant_check: rand_read_calls = count calls to crypto/rand.Read during one key generation; assert rand_read_calls >= 1; assert rand_read_calls <= MAX_EXPECTED_CALLS (e.g., 5); assert 'math/rand' not called; assert generated key_id matches ^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$ and secret matches ^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$

property_tests.description: For any write to the users table where all supplied field values equal stored values, updated_at retains its pre-write value after the operation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any user record, issue a PUT /api/v1/users/:id with a body where all fields match current stored values (no-op write); repeat for any subset of fields
property_tests.invariant_check: T0 = SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = :id; issue no-op PUT; T1 = SELECT updated_at FROM users WHERE id = :id; assert T1 == T0

property_tests.description: provider_id is immutable after initial creation; subsequent callback invocations for the same (provider, provider_id) never update the provider_id column.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any user created via POST /api/v1/auth/callback, perform multiple subsequent callback calls with the same (provider, provider_id) pair but different provider responses
property_tests.invariant_check: initial_provider_id = SELECT provider_id FROM users WHERE id = :user_id after first callback; for each subsequent callback: current_provider_id = SELECT provider_id FROM users WHERE id = :user_id; assert current_provider_id == initial_provider_id

edge_case_tests.description: GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with empty providers array when no providers are registered.
preconditions: Server initialized with an empty provider registry (no providers registered)
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/auth/providers (no auth required)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 200; body is {providers: []}
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert resp.status == 200
assert resp.body.providers == []

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with unrecognized provider returns HTTP 400 with structured error body.
preconditions: Server running; provider registry contains only 'github'
No code exchange is made to any external service
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'fakeprovider', code: 'code123', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; structured error body describes unrecognized provider; no external HTTP request made to any OAuth provider
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'fakeprovider', code:'code123', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 400
assert resp.body.error != None or resp.body.message != None
# Verify no external call was made (mock assertion)
assert mock_github_exchange.call_count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with invalid expires value returns HTTP 400 listing accepted values; does not proceed to code exchange.
preconditions: GitHub provider registered and exchange mock ready but not expected to be called
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'c', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb', expires: 45}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; error body lists accepted values [0, 30, 60, 90]; code exchange NOT called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb', expires:45})
assert resp.status == 400
err_body = resp.body
assert '0' in str(err_body) or '30' in str(err_body)
assert mock_github_exchange.call_count == 0
# Also test non-integer
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb', expires:'ninety'})
assert resp2.status == 400

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with non-allowlisted redirect_uri returns HTTP 400; code exchange not performed.
preconditions: Server running in production mode with external_url='https://app.example.com'
GitHub provider exchange mock ready but not expected to be called
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'c', redirect_uri: 'https://attacker.com/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; structured error body; no external OAuth provider request made
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'https://attacker.com/cb'})
assert resp.status == 400
assert mock_github_exchange.call_count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 502 when OAuth provider code exchange fails.
preconditions: GitHub provider token_url mocked to return HTTP 500 error
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'bad-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 502; structured error body signaling upstream provider failure; no DB writes (users table unchanged)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
user_count_before = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'bad-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 502
user_count_after = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
assert user_count_after == user_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 400 when provider returns null or empty email.
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return user info with email=null (or email='')
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'empty-email-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; structured error body; no user record created; no key created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
user_count_before = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'empty-email-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 400
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count == user_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 400 when derived username from GitHub fails validation rules.
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return user info with login='invalid user!', email='e@e.com'
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'invalid-login', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; structured error body describing validation failure; no user upsert or key creation
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
user_count_before = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'invalid-login', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 400
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count == user_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 409 when derived username conflicts case-insensitively with a different (provider, provider_id).
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return login='alice' (different case), email='e@e.com', id='new-id-111'
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'conflict-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 409; structured error body; no user upsert or key creation for new-id-111
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'conflict-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 409
# Verify no new user created for new-id-111
assert db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE provider_id = ?', 'new-id-111') == None

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 403 for a blocked user; no API key generated; user not reactivated.
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return id='blocked-333', email='b@e.com', login='blockeduser'
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'blocked-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body; no new key created; user status remains 'blocked'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
key_count_before = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE provider_id = ?)', 'blocked-333').count
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'blocked-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 403
key_count_after = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE provider_id = ?)', 'blocked-333').count
assert key_count_after == key_count_before
assert db.query('SELECT status FROM users WHERE provider_id = ?', 'blocked-333').status == 'blocked'

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 500 when external_url is absent in production mode; does not fall back to permissive allowlist.
preconditions: Server configured in production mode with external_url explicitly unset or empty
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'c', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; structured error body indicating configuration error; no permissive fallback
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 500
assert resp.body.error != None  # structured error body present

edge_case_tests.description: Database transaction failure during callback rolls back all operations and returns HTTP 500.
preconditions: GitHub provider mocked to return valid user info
Database write is injected to fail during key creation step (e.g., unique constraint violation injected on api_keys)
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback (valid inputs, but db write fails during transaction)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; structured error body; no user upsert committed; no key row inserted; database state unchanged from pre-request
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
inject_db_failure_on_key_insert()
user_count_before = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
key_count_before = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys').count
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 500
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count == user_count_before
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys').count == key_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 502 and cancels outbound request when provider code exchange times out.
preconditions: GitHub token_url mocked to delay response beyond server timeout threshold
Server configured with a short timeout for OAuth provider calls
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'timeout-code', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 502; structured error body; no DB writes; response received within a reasonable time (context deadline applied)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
start = now()
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'timeout-code', redirect_uri:'http://localhost:3000/cb'})
elapsed = now() - start
assert resp.status == 502
assert elapsed < TIMEOUT_THRESHOLD + small_buffer  # bounded by context deadline
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count == 0  # no writes

edge_case_tests.description: redirect_uri with host 'localhost.evil.com' is rejected in dev mode; it is not treated as a localhost match.
preconditions: Server running in dev mode (no external_url set)
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/auth/callback with body: {provider: 'github', code: 'c', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost.evil.com:3000/cb'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; structured error body; not treated as valid localhost URI
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c', redirect_uri:'http://localhost.evil.com:3000/cb'})
assert resp.status == 400
# Also test 'http://mylocalhost:3000/cb'
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider:'github', code:'c2', redirect_uri:'http://mylocalhost:3000/cb'})
assert resp2.status == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Admin POST /api/v1/users returns HTTP 400 for invalid username or empty provider_id; no user record created.
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/users with body: {username: 'bad user!', email: 'e@e.com', provider: 'github', provider_id: 'p1'}; then with empty provider_id
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 for invalid username; HTTP 400 for empty provider_id; no rows inserted in users table
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'bad user!', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p1'})
assert r1.status == 400
count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
assert count == 0
r2 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'validname', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:''})
assert r2.status == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Admin POST /api/v1/users returns HTTP 409 for case-insensitive username conflict or duplicate (provider, provider_id).
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/users with username='bob' (case conflict) or same (provider, provider_id)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 409 in both cases; no new user record created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
user_count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count
r1 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'bob', email:'e@e.com', provider:'gitlab', provider_id:'ext-300'})
assert r1.status == 409
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count == user_count
r2 = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'Unique', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'ext-200'})
assert r2.status == 409
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').count == user_count

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/users returns HTTP 403 when called by a non-admin authenticated user.
preconditions: Non-admin user with valid API key
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/users with non-admin user API key and valid body
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body; no user record created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'user-api-key'}, body={username:'newuser', email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p1'})
assert resp.status == 403
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE username = ?', 'newuser').count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: GET /api/v1/users returns HTTP 403 when called by a non-admin authenticated user.
preconditions: Non-admin user with valid API key
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/users with non-admin user API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'user-api-key'})
assert resp.status == 403

edge_case_tests.description: GET /api/v1/users/:id returns HTTP 404 with structured error body when user ID does not exist.
preconditions: No user with id='non-existent-uuid' in users table
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/users/non-existent-uuid with admin token
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404; structured error body in server_foundation format
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/users/non-existent-uuid', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 404
assert resp.body.error != None  # structured error body per server_foundation

edge_case_tests.description: GET /api/v1/users/:id returns HTTP 403 when called by a non-admin authenticated user.
preconditions: Non-admin user authenticated with API key
Target user 'user-uuid-target' exists in users table
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/users/user-uuid-target with non-admin user API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-target', headers={'Authorization':'non-admin-key'})
assert resp.status == 403

edge_case_tests.description: Non-admin PUT /api/v1/users/:id on another user's record returns HTTP 403; no update performed.
preconditions: User A ('user-uuid-a') authenticated with API key
edge_case_tests.input: PUT /api/v1/users/user-uuid-b with User A's key and body: {full_name: 'Hacked'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body; User B's full_name unchanged in DB
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-b', headers={'Authorization':'user-a-key'}, body={full_name:'Hacked'})
assert resp.status == 403
assert db.query('SELECT full_name FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-b').full_name == 'Original'

edge_case_tests.description: Non-admin PUT /api/v1/users/:id with status field returns HTTP 403 regardless of other fields.
edge_case_tests.input: PUT /api/v1/users/user-uuid-c with user's own key and body: {status: 'blocked', full_name: 'Test'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body; no update performed even for full_name
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
T0_fullname = db.query('SELECT full_name FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-c').full_name
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/user-uuid-c', headers={'Authorization':'user-c-key'}, body={status:'blocked', full_name:'Test'})
assert resp.status == 403
assert db.query('SELECT full_name, status FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-c').full_name == T0_fullname
assert db.query('SELECT status FROM users WHERE id = ?', 'user-uuid-c').status == 'active'

edge_case_tests.description: PUT /api/v1/users/:id returns HTTP 404 with structured error body when user ID does not exist.
preconditions: No user with id='missing-user-uuid' in users table
edge_case_tests.input: PUT /api/v1/users/missing-user-uuid with admin token and body: {full_name: 'Test'}
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404; structured error body in server_foundation format
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = PUT('/api/v1/users/missing-user-uuid', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={full_name:'Test'})
assert resp.status == 404
assert resp.body.error != None

edge_case_tests.description: GET /api/v1/keys returns HTTP 403 when called with a workspace token.
preconditions: A valid workspace token is available (as defined by server_foundation middleware)
edge_case_tests.input: GET /api/v1/keys with workspace token in Authorization header
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={'Authorization':'workspace-token'})
assert resp.status == 403

edge_case_tests.description: POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 404 for non-existent key_id or revoked key.
preconditions: Key 'key-nonexist' does not exist in api_keys table
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/keys/key-nonexist/refresh with admin token; POST /api/v1/keys/key-revoked/refresh with admin token
edge_case_tests.expected: Both return HTTP 404; structured error body per server_foundation format
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-nonexist/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert r1.status == 404
assert r1.body.error != None
r2 = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-revoked/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert r2.status == 404
assert r2.body.error != None

edge_case_tests.description: Non-admin POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh for another user's key returns HTTP 403; no secret rotated.
preconditions: User A authenticated with API key
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/keys/key-of-user-b/refresh with User A's API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body; secret_hash in DB for key-of-user-b unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
old_hash = db.query('SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-of-user-b').secret_hash
resp = POST('/api/v1/keys/key-of-user-b/refresh', headers={'Authorization':'user-a-key'})
assert resp.status == 403
assert db.query('SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-of-user-b').secret_hash == old_hash

edge_case_tests.description: DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id returns HTTP 404 when key_id does not exist in api_keys table.
preconditions: No row in api_keys with key_id='key-doesnotexist'
edge_case_tests.input: DELETE /api/v1/keys/key-doesnotexist with admin token
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404; structured error body per server_foundation format
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/keys/key-doesnotexist', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'})
assert resp.status == 404
assert resp.body.error != None

edge_case_tests.description: Non-admin DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id for another user's key returns HTTP 403; revoked_at not set.
preconditions: User A authenticated with API key
Key 'key-of-user-c' belongs to User C, revoked_at=null
edge_case_tests.input: DELETE /api/v1/keys/key-of-user-c with User A's API key
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403; structured error body; key-of-user-c.revoked_at remains null in DB
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = DELETE('/api/v1/keys/key-of-user-c', headers={'Authorization':'user-a-key'})
assert resp.status == 403
assert db.query('SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?', 'key-of-user-c').revoked_at == None

edge_case_tests.description: Username with spaces, underscores, or special characters returns HTTP 400; user record not created or updated.
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/users with username='alice_bob' (underscore), then 'alice bob' (space), then 'alice@bob' (special char)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 for all three; no user records created

edge_case_tests.description: Username exceeding 39 characters returns HTTP 400; user record not created.
edge_case_tests.input: POST /api/v1/users with username='a'*40 (40 chars)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; structured error body; no user record created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={'Authorization':'admin-token'}, body={username:'a'*40, email:'e@e.com', provider:'github', provider_id:'p1'})
assert resp.status == 400
assert db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE LENGTH(username) > 39').count == 0

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end GitHub OAuth login flow: provider listing, code exchange, user creation, and API key issuance complete successfully in a single request-response cycle.
smoke_tests.trigger: CLI calls GET /api/v1/auth/providers, constructs authorize URL, then calls POST /api/v1/auth/callback with a mocked GitHub code exchange returning valid user info
real_components: Echo HTTP server with all OAuth and user routes registered
SQLite database with users and api_keys tables (from server_foundation schema)
Provider registry with GitHub provider configured
mockable: GitHub OAuth token_url endpoint (exchange code for access token)
GitHub userinfo_url endpoint (return user profile with login, email, id)
expected_effects: GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with github provider including authorize_url and scopes='user:email'
POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 200 with user object (id is UUID v4, status='active') and api_key object (token matches af_<8chars>_<32chars>)
One new row inserted in users table with correct fields
One new row inserted in api_keys table with secret_hash=SHA-256(returned secret), expires_at approximately 90 days from now
No plaintext secret stored in database
GitHub mock endpoints called exactly once each

smoke_tests.description: Admin pre-provisions a user via POST /api/v1/users, then that user logs in via OAuth callback and receives an API key.
smoke_tests.trigger: Admin calls POST /api/v1/users with username, email, provider, provider_id; then the same (provider, provider_id) user authenticates via POST /api/v1/auth/callback
real_components: Echo HTTP server with user management and OAuth routes registered
SQLite database with users and api_keys tables
expected_effects: POST /api/v1/users returns HTTP 201 with user object excluding provider_id; no api_keys row created
Subsequent POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 200 with same user id (found by provider+provider_id match) and a new api_key with 90-day expiry
users table has exactly one row for this user after both calls
api_keys table has exactly one row for this user after login

smoke_tests.description: Authenticated user refreshes their own expired API key and receives a new secret without re-authenticating via OAuth.
smoke_tests.trigger: User calls POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh for their own expired (non-revoked) key
real_components: Echo HTTP server with key management routes
SQLite database with api_keys table containing an expired key (expires_at in the past, revoked_at=null)
server_foundation user API key auth middleware
mockable: No external services required for this path
expected_effects: POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh returns HTTP 200 with same key_id, new plaintext secret (32 alphanumeric chars), token in af_<key_id>_<new_secret> format, revoked_at=null
api_keys row has updated secret_hash = SHA-256(new_secret)
expires_at recalculated as now + original N days (not today + 90)
Old secret_hash no longer matches previously returned secret

smoke_tests.description: Admin blocks a user and revokes their API key; blocked user cannot subsequently log in via OAuth.
smoke_tests.trigger: Admin calls PUT /api/v1/users/:id with status='blocked', then DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id for the user's key; then blocked user attempts POST /api/v1/auth/callback
real_components: Echo HTTP server with user management, key management, and OAuth routes
SQLite database with users and api_keys tables
server_foundation user API key validation middleware
mockable: GitHub OAuth token_url endpoint (for the blocked user's subsequent login attempt)
GitHub userinfo_url endpoint (returns blocked user's provider_id)
expected_effects: PUT /api/v1/users/:id returns HTTP 200 with status='blocked'; updated_at bumped
DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id returns HTTP 204; api_keys row has revoked_at set
Subsequent POST /api/v1/auth/callback for the blocked user returns HTTP 403
No new API key created for blocked user
User's status remains 'blocked' (not reactivated by callback attempt)
